content.text:
Perfect! Now I have all the information needed. Let me compile a comprehensive summary.

## Jiranimo Repository Overview

### 1. Overall Directory Structure

```
/Users/niryo/git/Jiranimo/
├── extension/                 # Chrome extension files
│   ├── background.js         # Background service worker
│   ├── manifest.json         # Extension manifest (v3)
│   ├── content/              # Content scripts for Jira board pages
│   │   ├── content.js       # Main board interaction logic
│   │   ├── content.css      # Styles for overlays and badges
│   │   └── board-config.js  # Board configuration modal
│   ├── lib/                  # Utility libraries
│   │   ├── adf-to-markdown.js
│   │   └── jira-api.js
│   ├── options/              # Extension settings page
│   │   ├── options.html
│   │   └── options.js
│   ├── icons/                # Extension icons
│   └── package.json
├── server/                    # Node.js backend server
│   ├── src/
│   │   ├── index.ts         # Main server entry point
│   │   ├── web/              # Express web server & API
│   │   │   ├── server.ts
│   │   │   ├── api-routes.ts
│   │   │   ├── ws-handler.ts # WebSocket handler
│   │   │   └── public/       # Dashboard UI (served as static)
│   │   │       ├── index.html
│   │   │       ├── app.js    # Dashboard JavaScript
│   │   │       └── style.css
│   │   ├── pipeline/         # Task execution pipeline
│   │   ├── claude/           # Claude AI integration
│   │   ├── config/           # Configuration schemas
│   │   ├── state/            # Task state management
│   │   ├── mcp/              # Model Context Protocol
│   │   └── git/              # Git operations
│   └── test/
├── package.json              # Root workspace configuration
├── node_modules/             # Dependencies
└── scripts/                   # Build/dev scripts
```

### 2. Main Frontend Files (Extension Board Screen)

**Core Board Interaction:**
- `/Users/niryo/git/Jiranimo/extension/content/content.js` (21,987 bytes)
  - Main content script running on Jira sprint board pages
  - Scans cards for trigger labels via Jira REST API
  - Injects "Implement" badges with sparkles SVG icons
  - Handles badge clicks to submit tasks to the server
  - Polls/WebSocket syncs task status updates
  - Transitions Jira issues and posts completion comments

- `/Users/niryo/git/Jiranimo/extension/content/board-config.js` (6,283 bytes)
  - Guided board configuration modal (first-time setup)
  - Fetches board columns from Jira Agile API
  - Resolves workflow transitions for "In Progress" and "In Review"
  - Stores config per board in Chrome storage

**CSS & Styling:**
- `/Users/niryo/git/Jiranimo/extension/content/content.css` (2,569 bytes)
  - Styles for `.jiranimo-icon` badges with animations (idle, queued, in-progress, completed, failed)
  - Config overlay and modal styles (`.jiranimo-config-overlay`, `.jiranimo-config-modal`)
  - Animations: jiranimo-spin, jiranimo-pulse, jiranimo-sparkle

### 3. Overlay/Modal Components

**Configuration Modal (Board Setup):**
- Defined in `board-config.js` (lines 28-94)
- HTML structure: `div.jiranimo-config-overlay` > `div.jiranimo-config-modal`
- Modal contains dropdowns for selecting "In Progress" and "In Review" columns
- Styled in `content.css` (lines 64-121)
- Features:
  - Semi-transparent dark backdrop: `rgba(9, 30, 66, 0.54)`
  - 420px width modal, closes on backdrop click
  - Pre-selects likely columns (containing "progress", "done", "review")
  - High z-index (10000)

**Dashboard UI (Server):**
- `/Users/niryo/git/Jiranimo/server/src/web/public/index.html`
- Served as static dashboard at http://localhost:3456
- Displays tasks in 4 columns: Queued, In Progress, Completed, Failed
- WebSocket connection for real-time updates
- Task cards show: key, summary, priority, type, PR link, cost

### 4. Project Type & Package Files

**Project Type:** Browser Extension + Node.js Backend Monorepo

**Extension Manifest:**
- `/Users/niryo/git/Jiranimo/extension/manifest.json` (Manifest V3)
- Content scripts injected into: `https://*.atlassian.net/jira/software/projects/*/boards/*`
- Permissions: `storage`, `tabs`
- Host permissions for atlassian.net and localhost

**Root package.json:**
- `/Users/niryo/git/Jiranimo/package.json`
- Workspace configuration with `server` and `extension` workspaces
- Scripts for dev, testing, integration tests, and e2e tests
- Uses npm workspaces

**Server Dependencies:**
- Express.js (web framework)
- TypeScript/tsx (runtime)
- WebSocket support (ws)
- Zod (schema validation)
- Anthropic Claude API integration

### 5. Keyboard Shortcut Handling

**Current Status: NO KEYBOARD SHORTCUTS IMPLEMENTED**

Search results show NO keyboard event listeners (`keydown`, `keyup`, `keypress`, `keyboard`) in:
- `/Users/niryo/git/Jiranimo/extension/**/*.js`
- `/Users/niryo/git/Jiranimo/server/src/**/*.ts`

**Current Interaction Pattern:**
- All interactions are mouse-click based
- Badge injection in content script (line 327-331 in content.js):
  ```javascript
  icon.addEventListener('click', (e) => {
    e.preventDefault();
    e.stopPropagation();
    handleBadgeClick(icon, issueKey);
  });
  ```
- Modal interactions: dropdown selects + button click
- Dashboard: button click for "Retry Task"

**Key Files for Reference:**
- Configuration modal creation: `/Users/niryo/git/Jiranimo/extension/content/board-config.js:28-94`
- Badge state management: `/Users/niryo/git/Jiranimo/extension/content/content.js:417-445`
- Message handling: `/Users/niryo/git/Jiranimo/extension/content/content.js:552-592`

This is a mature project ready for keyboard shortcut enhancement. The modal infrastructure and event handling patterns are well-established and modular.

input.title: [SCRUM-5] add an about page overlay on cmd+e
input.head: jiranimo/SCRUM-5-about-page-overlay
input.body:
Implements SCRUM-5. Jira: https://jiranimoapp.atlassian.net/browse/SCRUM-5

## Changes

- **`content.js`**: Adds a `showAboutOverlay()` / `closeAboutOverlay()` function pair and a `keydown` listener that toggles the overlay on `Cmd+E`. `Escape` and backdrop click also close it.
- **`content.css`**: New `.jiranimo-about-overlay` and `.jiranimo-about-modal` styles with a fade-in animation, matching the existing config modal design language.

## What it shows

The overlay displays the extension name, version, a short description of what Jiranimo does, and a keyboard shortcuts reference table.
